tasks.title: Initialize Quarkus Gradle Project with GraalVM Native Support
tasks.description: Create Quarkus project with Gradle build configuration, GraalVM native compilation setup, and reactive extensions for 64MB memory constraint
tasks.details:
1. Initialize Quarkus 3.6.4+ project using Gradle 8.5+
2. Configure build.gradle with Quarkus platform BOM and dependencies:
   - quarkus-resteasy-reactive-jackson
   - quarkus-hibernate-reactive-panache
   - quarkus-reactive-mysql-client
   - quarkus-reactive-pg-client (for SQLite via reactive-pg-client)
   - quarkus-vertx
   - quarkus-smallrye-jwt
   - jackson-dataformat-yaml (SnakeYAML Engine)
3. Configure native image build settings in build.gradle:
   - quarkus.native.additional-build-args: -H:+ReportExceptionStackTraces, --gc=serial, -march=armv7-a
   - Set memory limits: -Xmx64m, -Xms32m
4. Setup project structure: src/main/java/com/iptvorganizer/{controllers,services,models,migrations,config}
5. Create application.properties with initial Quarkus configuration
6. Verify Gradle build executes successfully: ./gradlew clean build
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Run './gradlew build' to verify project compiles
2. Execute './gradlew build -Dquarkus.package.type=native' to test native compilation
3. Verify generated native executable size and startup time
4. Confirm project structure matches package hierarchy

tasks.title: Implement Custom SimpleMigrator Database Migration System
tasks.description: Build lightweight custom migration system to save 3-4MB memory vs Flyway, with version tracking and checksum validation
tasks.details:
1. Create SimpleMigrator class in com.iptvorganizer.migrations package
2. Implement schema_version table for tracking applied migrations:
   - Fields: id, version, description, checksum, applied_at
3. Design migration file naming: V001__create_admin_users.sql, V002__create_sources.sql, etc.
4. Implement migration logic:
   - Scan resources/db/migration/{mysql,sqlite} for .sql files
   - Calculate MD5 checksum for each migration file
   - Compare with schema_version table to detect pending migrations
   - Execute pending migrations in order
   - Record successful migrations in schema_version
5. Support both MySQL and SQLite dialects
6. Add @ApplicationScoped bean for startup execution
7. Implement Mutiny reactive database access for migrations
8. NO rollback support (per PRD requirements)
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit tests: verify checksum calculation, migration ordering
2. Integration tests: apply migrations to test MySQL and SQLite databases
3. Test idempotency: re-run migrations, verify no duplicates
4. Validate schema_version table populated correctly
5. Memory profiling: confirm <4MB overhead vs Flyway

tasks.title: Design and Implement 11 Database Schemas with Panache Reactive Entities
tasks.description: Create MySQL and SQLite migration files for all 11 tables, implement Panache reactive entities with optimized connection pooling (2 connections max)
tasks.details:
1. Create migration SQL files in resources/db/migration/mysql/ and resources/db/migration/sqlite/:
   - V001__create_admin_users.sql (id, username, password_hash, created_at, updated_at)
   - V002__create_sources.sql (id, name, url, username, password, sync_interval, enableproxy, disablestreamproxy, stream_follow_location, created_at, updated_at)
   - V003__create_filters.sql (id, source_id, yaml_config, created_at, updated_at)
   - V004__create_clients.sql (id, source_id, username, password, expiry_date, block_adult, created_at, updated_at)
   - V005__create_categories.sql (id, source_id, category_id, category_name, parent_id, type, created_at, updated_at)
   - V006__create_live_streams.sql (id, source_id, stream_id, name, category_id, stream_icon, epg_channel_id, created_at, updated_at)
   - V007__create_vod_streams.sql (id, source_id, stream_id, name, category_id, stream_icon, created_at, updated_at)
   - V008__create_series.sql (id, source_id, series_id, name, category_id, cover, created_at, updated_at)
   - V009__create_sync_logs.sql (id, source_id, status, message, started_at, completed_at)
   - V010__create_connection_logs.sql (id, client_id, ip_address, user_agent, connected_at)
   - V011__create_sync_schedule.sql (id, source_id, next_sync_time, created_at, updated_at)
2. Create Panache reactive entities extending PanacheEntityBase:
   - @Entity classes: AdminUser, Source, Filter, Client, Category, LiveStream, VodStream, Series, SyncLog, ConnectionLog, SyncSchedule
   - Use @Id @GeneratedValue for primary keys
   - Add @CreationTimestamp and @UpdateTimestamp for timestamps
   - Implement reactive queries using Mutiny Uni/Multi
3. Configure reactive database pool in application.properties:
   - quarkus.datasource.reactive.max-size=2
   - quarkus.datasource.reactive.idle-timeout=30s
4. Support MySQL (quarkus-reactive-mysql-client) and SQLite (via reactive-pg-client configuration)
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Run SimpleMigrator to apply all 11 migrations to MySQL test database
2. Run SimpleMigrator to apply all 11 migrations to SQLite test database
3. Unit tests for each Panache entity: CRUD operations using reactive APIs
4. Verify foreign key constraints (source_id, client_id)
5. Test connection pool: confirm max 2 connections under load
6. Memory profiling: database pool overhead <8MB

tasks.title: Build Core Streaming Components (StreamingJsonParser, HttpStreamingService, FilterService)
tasks.description: Implement memory-efficient streaming JSON parser, HTTP client with zero-copy streaming, and YAML filter engine with regex matching
tasks.details:
1. StreamingJsonParser (com.iptvorganizer.services.StreamingJsonParser):
   - Use Jackson Streaming API (JsonParser with JsonFactory)
   - Process JSON in 128KB chunks
   - Emit items via Mutiny Multi<T>
   - Explicit GC call every 1000 items: System.gc()
   - Handle large Xtream API responses (categories, streams)
2. HttpStreamingService (com.iptvorganizer.services.HttpStreamingService):
   - Use Vert.x WebClient for reactive HTTP requests
   - Implement zero-copy streaming with Multi<Buffer>
   - Configure 8-16KB buffer chunks
   - Support backpressure to prevent memory overflow
   - Handle upstream timeouts and retries
3. FilterService (com.iptvorganizer.services.FilterService):
   - Parse YAML filter configs using SnakeYAML Engine
   - Implement include/exclude regex pattern matching
   - Cache compiled Pattern objects per filter
   - Apply filters to stream lists (favoris system)
   - Support adult content filtering flag
4. All services must be @ApplicationScoped reactive beans
5. Integrate with ProxyConfigService for upstream proxy support
tasks.testStrategy:
1. StreamingJsonParser: parse 10K item JSON, verify memory <30MB, time <3s
2. HttpStreamingService: stream 100MB video file, verify constant 16KB memory usage
3. FilterService: load 50 filters with 200 regex patterns, verify <10MB memory
4. Integration test: stream and filter Xtream API response with 5000 items
5. Load test: 50 concurrent HTTP streams for 10 minutes, verify no memory leaks

tasks.title: Implement Background Sync Service with Quarkus Scheduler
tasks.description: Build reactive sync daemon using Quarkus Scheduler with batch processing (100 items/batch), explicit GC calls, and transaction safety
tasks.details:
1. Create SyncService (@ApplicationScoped) with Quarkus Scheduler:
   - @Scheduled(cron = "0 */5 * * * ?") for periodic checks
   - Configurable via application.properties: sync.check.interval
2. Implement sync workflow:
   - Query Source entities for sources needing sync (next_sync_time < now)
   - For each source: fetch categories, live streams, VOD, series from upstream Xtream API
   - Use StreamingJsonParser for memory-efficient parsing
   - Apply FilterService to filter content per source filters
3. Batch processing:
   - Process streams in batches of 100 items
   - Use Panache Reactive transactions: Panache.withTransaction()
   - Explicit System.gc() after each batch
4. Implement LabelExtractor:
   - Extract labels from stream names using regex patterns
   - Store extracted labels in stream metadata
5. Create SyncLog entries:
   - Record sync start, completion, errors
   - Update SyncSchedule.next_sync_time after successful sync
6. Handle concurrent sync prevention using database locks or Redis
7. Use Mutiny Multi for asynchronous processing
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit test: sync single source with 1000 streams
2. Memory profiling during sync: verify <58MB RSS
3. Test batch processing: confirm 100 items/batch, GC called between batches
4. Integration test: sync 5 sources concurrently, verify no deadlocks
5. Load test: sync 10K streams, verify completion time and memory usage
6. Verify SyncLog and SyncSchedule tables updated correctly

tasks.title: Implement Complete Admin REST API with JWT Authentication
tasks.description: Build 9 RESTEasy Reactive controllers for admin UI: Auth, Sources, Clients, Filters, Categories, Streams, Sync, ConnectionLogs, Dashboard
tasks.details:
1. AuthController (com.iptvorganizer.controllers.admin.AuthController):
   - POST /api/auth/login: validate username/password, return JWT token
   - POST /api/auth/logout: invalidate token (blacklist or short expiry)
   - Use SmallRye JWT for token generation/validation
2. SourceController:
   - GET /api/sources: list all sources (paginated)
   - GET /api/sources/{id}: get source details
   - POST /api/sources: create source (with proxy fields: enableproxy, disablestreamproxy, stream_follow_location)
   - PUT /api/sources/{id}: update source
   - DELETE /api/sources/{id}: delete source
   - GET /api/sources/{id}/sync-status: get sync status
   - POST /api/sources/{id}/test-connection: test upstream connection
3. ClientController, FilterController, CategoriesController, StreamsController, SyncController, ConnectionLogsController, DashboardController:
   - Implement CRUD endpoints per PRD specification
   - All endpoints require @RolesAllowed("admin") JWT authentication
4. Use Mutiny Uni<Response> for reactive responses
5. Implement JSON serialization with Jackson
6. Add CORS configuration for React admin UI
7. Error handling: map exceptions to HTTP status codes
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit tests for each controller endpoint (20+ endpoints)
2. Integration tests with test database
3. JWT token validation tests: valid, expired, invalid tokens
4. Test React admin UI integration: all CRUD operations
5. Performance: API response time p99 <100ms
6. Test pagination, filtering, sorting for list endpoints

tasks.title: Implement Xtream Codes API and Client-Facing Proxy System
tasks.description: Build XtreamController with full Xtream API compatibility and StreamDataController with dual proxy architecture for handling 302 redirects
tasks.details:
1. XtreamController (com.iptvorganizer.controllers.XtreamController):
   - GET /player_api.php: server info and authentication
   - GET /player_api.php?action=get_live_categories: list live categories
   - GET /player_api.php?action=get_vod_categories: list VOD categories
   - GET /player_api.php?action=get_series_categories: list series categories
   - GET /player_api.php?action=get_live_streams: list live streams
   - GET /player_api.php?action=get_vod_streams: list VOD streams
   - GET /player_api.php?action=get_series: list series
   - Validate client credentials (username/password) for each request
   - Apply FilterService to filter content per client's source
2. StreamDataController (com.iptvorganizer.controllers.StreamDataController):
   - GET /live/{username}/{password}/{stream_id}.{ext}: live TV streaming
   - GET /movie/{username}/{password}/{stream_id}.{ext}: VOD streaming
   - GET /series/{username}/{password}/{stream_id}.{ext}: series streaming
   - Handle 302 redirects from upstream:
     * If disablestreamproxy=true: return 302 directly to client
     * If disablestreamproxy=false: encode redirect URL in base64, return 302 to /proxy/{user}/{pass}?url={base64}
   - Direct streaming when no redirect: use HttpStreamingService
3. ProxyController (com.iptvorganizer.controllers.ProxyController):
   - GET /proxy/{username}/{password}?url={base64_encoded_url}: decode and stream
   - Follow additional redirects based on stream_follow_location flag
   - Use HttpStreamingService for zero-copy streaming
4. EPG Support:
   - GET /live/xmltv.php: return EPG data in XMLTV format
5. All responses must comply with Xtream Codes API JSON format
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test with VLC: play live stream, VOD, series
2. Test with Kodi: verify Xtream plugin compatibility
3. Test with TiviMate, GSE IPTV: verify client compatibility
4. Test 302 redirect scenarios: direct, via proxy, bypass proxy
5. Functional tests: all Xtream API endpoints with valid/invalid credentials
6. Performance: stream latency <200ms for first byte
7. Load test: 50 concurrent streams for 4 hours

tasks.title: Implement ProxyConfigService for Upstream Proxy Support (HTTP/HTTPS/SOCKS5)
tasks.description: Build centralized proxy configuration service with global and per-source settings, authentication support, and integration with HttpStreamingService
tasks.details:
1. Create ProxyConfigService (@ApplicationScoped):
   - Load configuration from environment variables at startup
   - Global settings: PROXY_ENABLED, PROXY_URL, PROXY_TYPE, PROXY_HOST, PROXY_PORT, PROXY_USERNAME, PROXY_PASSWORD
   - Support unified URL format: http://user:pass@proxy.example.com:8080
   - Support component-based format: separate host, port, username, password
2. Implement proxy type detection:
   - HTTP/HTTPS proxy
   - SOCKS5 proxy
3. Per-source proxy override:
   - Check Source.enableproxy field (null/true/false)
   - If false: bypass proxy for this source
   - If null/true: use global proxy if enabled
4. Integrate with Vert.x WebClient:
   - Configure ProxyOptions for WebClient
   - Support proxy authentication (username/password)
5. Provide configuration methods:
   - getProxyConfig(Source source): ProxyConfig
   - isProxyEnabled(Source source): boolean
6. Validate configuration on startup:
   - If PROXY_USERNAME set, PROXY_PASSWORD must be set
   - If PROXY_HOST set, PROXY_PORT must be set
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit tests: parse PROXY_URL, component-based config
2. Test HTTP proxy: route upstream requests through HTTP proxy
3. Test HTTPS proxy: route requests through HTTPS proxy
4. Test SOCKS5 proxy: route requests through SOCKS5 proxy
5. Test proxy authentication: username/password validation
6. Test per-source override: source with enableproxy=false bypasses proxy
7. Integration test: sync sources via proxy, stream video via proxy

tasks.title: Create GitHub Actions Build & Test Workflow
tasks.description: Create java-build-test.yml workflow for automated Java build and test on every push and PR to java branch
tasks.details:
1. Create .github/workflows/java-build-test.yml:
   - Trigger: push and pull requests on java branch only
   - Setup JDK 17 (Temurin distribution) with Gradle cache enabled
   - Build with Gradle:
     * Command: cd java && ./gradlew build -Dquarkus.package.type=native
     * Compile Java sources and run linting/analysis
   - Run tests:
     * Command: cd java && ./gradlew test
     * Execute all unit and integration tests
   - Upload test results as artifacts:
     * Path: java/build/test-results/test/
     * Artifact name: test-results
   - Upload code coverage to codecov:
     * File: java/build/reports/jacoco/test/jacocoTestReport.xml
     * Flags: java-unittests
     * fail_ci_if_error: false
2. Ensure workflow fails if build or tests fail
3. Generate detailed test reports for debugging
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Push code to java branch, verify workflow triggers
2. Verify build completes successfully
3. Verify all tests pass
4. Check test results uploaded as artifacts
5. Verify codecov integration working
6. Test on PR: verify workflow runs on pull requests

tasks.title: Create GitHub Actions Docker Build & Push Workflow
tasks.description: Create java-docker-build.yml workflow for multi-architecture Docker image builds and GHCR publishing
tasks.details:
1. Create .github/workflows/java-docker-build.yml:
   - Trigger: push to java branch, tags matching java-v*, PRs to java branch
   - Setup JDK 17 (Temurin distribution) with Gradle cache
   - Build Java with Gradle native compilation:
     * Command: cd java && ./gradlew build -Dquarkus.package.type=native --no-daemon
     * Produces GraalVM native executable (*-runner)
   - Setup Docker Buildx for multi-platform builds
   - Login to GHCR (GitHub Container Registry):
     * Skip on pull requests
     * Use GitHub Actions token for authentication
   - Extract metadata and generate image tags:
     * Branch tag: ghcr.io/anasoid/iptv-organizer-proxy:java
     * Semantic version tags from java-v* git tags (e.g., java-v1.2.3)
     * Latest tag: java-latest for java branch
   - Build and push Docker image:
     * Multi-platforms: linux/amd64, linux/arm/v7, linux/arm64
     * Use GitHub Actions cache for faster rebuilds (BuildKit cache)
     * Push only on successful builds to java branch (skip on PRs)
   - Generate build summary with image tags and platforms
2. Dockerfile location: ./Dockerfile (root directory, already created)
3. Use BuildKit cache optimization for faster builds
4. Support semantic versioning via git tags
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Push to java branch, verify Docker build workflow triggers
2. Verify Gradle native build completes successfully
3. Verify Docker image builds for all platforms (amd64, arm/v7, arm64)
4. Verify image pushed to GHCR with correct tags
5. Pull image from GHCR and verify it runs: docker run ghcr.io/anasoid/iptv-organizer-proxy:java
6. Test git tag triggering: create java-v1.0.0 tag, verify image published with that tag
7. Test image size <80MB
8. Verify health check endpoint works on running container

tasks.title: Configuration Management and Application Properties
tasks.description: Convert .env configuration to application.yaml, configure database connections, memory limits, JWT secrets, CORS, and sync scheduler intervals
tasks.details:
1. Create src/main/resources/application.yaml:
   - Database configuration:
     * quarkus.datasource.db-kind: mysql or postgresql (for SQLite emulation)
     * quarkus.datasource.reactive.url: jdbc:mysql://localhost:3306/iptv_proxy
     * quarkus.datasource.username/password from env vars
   - Hibernate configuration:
     * quarkus.hibernate-orm.database.generation: none (use SimpleMigrator)
   - Reactive pool settings:
     * quarkus.datasource.reactive.max-size: 2
     * quarkus.datasource.reactive.idle-timeout: 30s
   - Memory settings:
     * quarkus.native.additional-build-args: -Xmx64m,-Xms32m,--gc=serial
   - JWT configuration:
     * mp.jwt.verify.issuer: iptv-organizer-proxy
     * smallrye.jwt.sign.key.location: from env var JWT_SECRET
   - CORS configuration:
     * quarkus.http.cors: true
     * quarkus.http.cors.origins: http://localhost:3000 (React admin UI)
   - Sync scheduler:
     * sync.check.interval: 300 (5 minutes)
     * sync.default.interval: 3600 (1 hour)
     * sync.lock.timeout: 1800 (30 minutes)
   - Proxy configuration:
     * proxy.enabled, proxy.url, proxy.type, proxy.host, proxy.port, proxy.username, proxy.password (from env vars)
2. Support environment variable overrides: %dev, %prod profiles
3. Create application-dev.yaml and application-prod.yaml for environment-specific configs
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Verify configuration loading: quarkus.log.level=DEBUG
2. Test database connection with MySQL and SQLite
3. Test JWT token generation with configured secret
4. Test CORS: preflight requests from React admin UI origin
5. Test sync scheduler: verify cron execution at configured interval
6. Test proxy configuration: verify ProxyConfigService loads settings
7. Verify environment variable overrides work correctly

tasks.title: Performance Testing, Memory Profiling, and Optimization
tasks.description: Validate memory usage targets (idle <45MB, load <60MB), conduct load testing (50 concurrent streams, 4 hours), and optimize for OpenWrt deployment
tasks.details:
1. Memory profiling using JFR (Java Flight Recorder):
   - Start application with JFR: -XX:StartFlightRecording=filename=recording.jfr
   - Idle state profiling: verify RSS <45MB
   - Load profiling: 3 concurrent video streams, verify RSS <60MB
   - Sync profiling: background sync running, verify RSS <58MB
2. Load testing using JMeter or Gatling:
   - Simulate 50 concurrent IPTV clients streaming video
   - Run for 4 hours continuously
   - Monitor memory usage, CPU usage, network throughput
   - Verify no OOM errors, no memory leaks
3. Performance benchmarking:
   - Startup time: measure from container start to ready state, target <2s
   - API response time: measure p50, p95, p99 for all endpoints, target p99 <100ms
   - Video stream latency: measure time to first byte, target <200ms
   - JSON parsing: parse 10K item response, target <3s
4. Optimization:
   - Tune GC parameters: -XX:+UseSerialGC for minimal footprint
   - Optimize native image: --initialize-at-build-time for static initialization
   - Reduce reflection usage: add reflection-config.json for GraalVM
   - Remove unused dependencies: analyze build output, exclude transitive deps
5. OpenWrt hardware validation:
   - Deploy to OpenWrt device (ARMv7 or ARM64)
   - Verify Docker container runs successfully
   - Test full functionality: Admin API, Xtream API, streaming
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Memory profiling: verify idle <45MB, load <60MB, sync <58MB
2. Load test results: 50 streams for 4 hours, no OOM, no memory leaks
3. Startup time: verify <2s on target hardware
4. API performance: p99 <100ms for all endpoints
5. Stream latency: <200ms time to first byte
6. JSON parsing: 10K items in <3s
7. OpenWrt deployment: successful deployment and full functional testing
8. Compare performance with PHP version: match or exceed throughput

tasks.title: Documentation and Release Preparation
tasks.description: Create comprehensive documentation including deployment instructions, configuration reference, memory tuning guide, API documentation, and release notes
tasks.details:
1. Deployment documentation (DEPLOYMENT.md):
   - Docker installation instructions
   - docker-compose.yml configuration for OpenWrt
   - Environment variable reference
   - Database setup (MySQL and SQLite)
   - Initial admin user creation
   - Health check and monitoring setup
2. Configuration reference (CONFIGURATION.md):
   - application.yaml structure and options
   - Database connection settings
   - Memory and GC tuning parameters
   - JWT configuration
   - Proxy configuration (global and per-source)
   - CORS settings
   - Sync scheduler configuration
3. Memory tuning guide (MEMORY-TUNING.md):
   - Memory budget breakdown (64MB heap)
   - GC tuning for OpenWrt
   - Native image optimization techniques
   - Profiling tools and commands
   - Troubleshooting OOM errors
4. API documentation (API.md):
   - Admin REST API: all endpoints with request/response examples
   - Xtream Codes API: all endpoints with Xtream format compliance
   - Authentication flow (JWT)
   - Error codes and responses
5. Release notes (RELEASE-NOTES.md):
   - Version information
   - Features implemented
   - Performance metrics (startup time, memory usage, throughput)
   - Breaking changes from PHP version
   - Known limitations
   - Migration guide (if applicable)
6. Developer guide (DEVELOPER.md):
   - Build instructions
   - Testing strategy
   - Architecture overview
   - Code structure and conventions
   - Contributing guidelines
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Review documentation completeness: all features documented
2. Test deployment instructions: deploy to fresh OpenWrt device
3. Validate configuration examples: copy-paste test
4. Test API examples: execute all documented API calls
5. Peer review: have team members review documentation
6. Generate OpenAPI/Swagger spec for Admin REST API
7. Verify release notes accuracy: test all listed features
